item.title: Saturday Sessions: Peter Frampton performs "Lyin'"
item.description: Peter Frampton is best known for his smash hits "Show Me the Way" and "Do You Feel Like I Do." Now, the multi-Grammy Award-winning guitarist and singer-songwriter is out with his first original album in 16 years, "Carry the Light." Here's Peter Frampton with "Lyin'."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Peter Frampton performs "Buried Treasure"
item.description: Peter Frampton is best known for his smash hits "Show Me the Way" and "Do You Feel Like I Do." Now, the multi-Grammy Award-winning guitarist and singer-songwriter is out with his first original album in 16 years, "Carry the Light." Here's Peter Frampton with "Buried Treasure."

item.title: Trump decision on Iran deal yet to come as Hegseth talks negotiations
item.description: President Trump held a meeting with key advisers on Friday to hammer out a "final determination" on a potential deal with Iran, but no word has come on a decision.

item.title: Jill Biden says she "truly supported" Joe Biden's pardon of their son Hunter
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden sits down with "CBS Sunday Morning," where she discusses everything from former President Joe Biden's health during his presidency to their son Hunter's pardon. Tune in for the full interview Sunday at 9 a.m. ET on CBS.

item.title: Blue Origin explosion sparks space race concerns
item.description: Blue Origin is assessing damage after a rocket exploded during a test run in Florida on Thursday.

item.title: Uncertainty remains over Iran ceasefire extension after Trump's marathon White House meeting
item.description: President Trump signaled Friday before a marathon meeting at the White House that he would make a final decision on the war with Iran, but uncertainty remains over where things stand after he failed to announce a deal or ceasefire extension after the meeting.

item.title: 4 more Laos miners freed from cave after being trapped for 10 days, rescuers say
item.description: Four more Laos miners who were trapped in a cave for 10 days were freed, rescuers say, while two remain missing.

item.title: Kouri Richins: Behind the Facade | Post Mortem
item.description: "48 Hours" correspondents Anne-Marie Green and Natalie Morales discuss the case of Kouri Richins, a Utah mom who was sentenced to life in prison without parole for murdering her husband, on Post Mortem after two trial jurors break down their take on the evidence.

item.title: Hegseth tones down warnings about China in visit to region
item.description: Last year, Hegseth called China a "threat" to Taiwan and said an attack might be "imminent."

item.title: Woman diagnosed with cancer after minor symptoms: "Couldn't believe it"
item.description: Candace Tucker thought her symptoms were benign. A colonoscopy led to an alarming diagnosis.

item.title: Trump call with Taiwanese president on hold
item.description: When Trump visited China earlier this month, Chinese President Xi Jinping warned him that Taiwan could become a "very dangerous situation" if mishandled.

item.title: Four more Laos gold miners rescued from flooded cave
item.description: A Laos rescue organization said​ that the water level inside the cave had receded enough for the four miners to leave with divers.

item.title: White House says Trump is in "excellent health" in results from physical
item.description: President Trump's physician said in a letter released Friday that the president is in "excellent health," following a physical earlier this week at Walter Reed National Military Hospital.

item.title: 5 killed, dozens injured when bus plows into several vehicles in Virginia
item.description: The five deaths came in vehicles that were struck by the bus when it did not slow down for traffic, Virginia State Police said.

item.title: At least 3 killed and 5 injured in Dallas explosion, officials say
item.description: The number of victims could change as details continue to unfold and crews search through the debris.

item.title: Death toll from U.S. strikes on alleged drug boats climbs above 200
item.description: The strikes are part of a monthslong campaign against alleged drug boats traversing the Caribbean Sea and eastern Pacific.

item.title: Trump directs CDC to align with assessment calling for fewer childhood vaccines
item.description: Earlier this year, the CDC announced updated recommendations that would reduce the number of recommended immunizations for children from 17 to 11.

item.title: Lead prosecutor leaves DOJ's case accusing James Comey of threatening Trump
item.description: The federal prosecutor who signed an indictment accusing former FBI Director James Comey of threatening President Trump by posting an image of seashells arranged as "86 47" is no longer on the case.

item.title: When a Texas man was forced to give up his dog, a fire station gave him a home
item.description: When a homeless man had no choice but to give up his dog, Jake, a local fire station gave Jake a home, and then helped the man get back on his feet.

item.title: 2026 summer will be worst yet for teen employment in decades, WSJ predicts
item.description: The Wall Street Journal reports that this summer is expected to be the worst for teen jobs since 1948, when the federal government started tracking the data. Ray A. Smith, a reporter covering career and workplace issues at the Wall Street Journal, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Who to know ahead of Colombia's election, and how it will impact U.S.
item.description: Voters in Colombia will head to the polls on Sunday to elect a new president. The winner could cause a dramatic shift in the country's politics for the next four years, but the impact reaches far beyond its borders. CBS News national correspondent Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: Key U.S. general meets with Cuban military near Guantanamo Bay
item.description: The head of the U.S. military's Southern Command met Friday with top Cuban military officials at the edge of Guantanamo Bay, a rare meeting as President Trump heaps pressure on Cuba and does not rule out military action.

item.title: Judge blocks closure of Kennedy Center, orders removal of Trump's name
item.description: A judge blocked the Kennedy Center from closing its doors during renovations, and ruled that its board acted unlawfully by adding President Trump's name to the building. The president reacted by saying he wants Congress to take it over.

item.title: Blue Origin assesses impact of New Glenn explosion
item.description: Blue Origin assess the impact of Thursday's New Glenn explosion, prompting concern about NASA moon program delays.

item.title: Diver who helped rescue miner in flooded Laos cave says it was a "trust-me dive"
item.description: One Laos gold miner was brought out of a flooded cave in a perilous two-hour operation where monsoon rains have trapped a group for over a week.

item.title: 7 of 9 bodies recovered after chemical tank implosion at paper mill
item.description: The tank ruptured Tuesday at the Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. facility in Longview, a city located along the southern Washington border with Oregon, killing 11 people.

item.title: Judge orders Trump to respond to claims "anti-weaponization fund" deal was "fraud"
item.description: A judge signaled she may reopen a case between President Trump and his own government that led the DOJ to create a $1.776 billion "anti-weaponization fund," ordering the president's lawyers to respond to allegations of "deception" and "fraud."

item.title: 5/29: CBS Evening News
item.description: A Virginia bus crash kills at least 5 and injures 44; divers rescue the first miner trapped in a cave.

item.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine bracing for "big attacks" by Russia in next 24 hours
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Ukraine has received intelligence indicating Russia will launch an assault involving drones and missiles.

item.title: ICE agent accused of shooting man in north Minneapolis arrested in Texas
item.description: An Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent accused of shooting a man in the leg in north Minneapolis and then lying about the attack​ was arrested in Texas Friday morning, according to the Hennepin County Attorney's Office.
